summary: This cluster of papers is connected by a shared focus on experimental methodologies for studying the ecological impacts of ocean acidification on benthic marine organisms. The research centers on the design and application of a continuous-flow, on-site mesocosm system that precisely manipulates carbonate chemistry, specifically targeting a broad range of seawater pH (from 8.05 to 7.28) and pCO2 levels (from 375 to 2691 μatm). Key findings demonstrate the system’s ability to stabilize these parameters through a controlled dilution of CO2-saturated seawater, enabling realistic simulations of future acidification scenarios. The geographic and ecological scope is broadly applicable to coastal benthic communities, with the method validated for in situ experimentation on organisms such as corals, calcifying algae, and other sessile invertebrates.
